what_it_does: A posted noticeboard tip that records a bidirectional link between output and source, referencing two image assets.
observations: Front matter declares id item-62207a65, type tip, channel provider-registry, state posted.
Links list references two image targets: image://img-b1e6e2c7 and image://img-e37625e8.
History shows a single state transition from null to posted by session ch-3mpkjg3r.
Title is 'ref' and source is claude_code.
standouts: Extremely terse body with an explicit bidirectional arrow symbol.
References two image assets without inline explanation.
Posted as a tip in provider-registry channel.
conventions: Uses YAML front matter with id, address, type, state, timestamps, history, and links.
Follows the noticeboard item schema with created/updated timestamps and a history transition.
Uses address URI scheme board:// and link targets with image:// scheme.
concerns: The body gives no context about which output/source or why the link matters.
No labels or captions on the referenced images, so the relationship is opaque from this file alone.
notes: A minimal cross-reference note pointing two images at an output/source linkage idea, but it says almost nothing on its own.
questions: What specific output and source are being linked?
What do the two referenced images depict?
Why is this filed as a tip in provider-registry?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated title 'ref' and body 'linking output↔source' match a reference note, but the file does not actually explain the linkage, so it functions more as a placeholder or pointer than a complete tip.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to flag or annotate a correspondence between generated output and its source material, using referenced images as evidence or examples.
novelty: The use of a literal bidirectional arrow in a one-line body to stand in for a full explanation is unusually concise.
contribution: A lightweight, timestamped pointer that records an intended output-to-source relationship and anchors it to two image references.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard tip 'ref' records a terse bidirectional output-to-source linkage and references two image assets in the provider-registry channel.
intention_for_embedding: To flag or annotate a relationship between some output and its source using linked images as supporting artifacts.
